name: wame
description: Claude Code plugins by WAME — developer productivity tools for teams

plugins.name: git-commit
plugins.description: Turn the current working tree into one or more logical git commits using the TYPE(scope): Message convention from the user's global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md. Title and body always in English; TYPE and scope inferred from the changes. When the run is tied to a task, the task ID is appended in square brackets — TYPE(scope)[<task-id>]: Message — detected from $ARGUMENTS, --task=<id>, or current branch name (feature/teamwork-task-<id>, task/<id>, ...). When no task ID is detected, the brackets are omitted entirely (never empty []). Groups related changes into one commit each; pass --all-in-one to skip grouping. Never adds Co-Authored-By lines, never pushes, never amends. Singular 'git-commit' — distinct from the existing 'git-commits' plugin which summarises historical commits.

plugins.name: git-commits
plugins.description: Fetch and summarize git commits across all repositories for a date range. Shows timeline with AI-powered summaries, file changes, and project breakdown. Supports GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket.

plugins.name: laravel-docs
plugins.description: Generate Laravel project specifications (technical, business, admin navigation) for individual models or modules. Auto-detects modular (wamesk/*, Modules/*) vs flat (app/Models) project structure. Configurable output paths, per-project config, and replaceable prompts per documentation type.

plugins.name: teamwork-tasks-from-dnr
plugins.description: Generate Teamwork.com import-ready task plan (XLSX + Markdown) from a 'Detailný návrh riešenia' (DNR) document. LLM-powered extraction of tasklists, business-friendly task names, acceptance criteria, and technical plans. Multi-language (sk/en/cs). Works as Claude Code plugin and Claude.ai Skill with the same SKILL.md.

plugins.name: teamwork-task
plugins.description: Fetch Teamwork.com tasklist/task by URL (including all task comments), render a plan for approval, then implement each task in the current repository, commit per task using TYPE(scope)[<task-id>]: Message convention, and log spent time back to Teamwork via the REST API v3 as sequential, non-overlapping entries (both start time and duration aligned to the 5-minute rounding step). Time-log descriptions are written in a business tone (what was delivered for the user/business), technical detail only when needed for identification. Parses task descriptions on the first HR into acceptance criteria + final summary. Configurable plan mode (overview | per_task | none), time tracking (real time rounded to 5 minutes, or ask) and branching strategy (current branch or new feature branch). Persistent config (API token survives plugin updates). Pauses and asks via AskUserQuestion on blockers. Push is left to the user.
